rulings.Case Title: KAYCIE CROSSLEY, AN INDIVIDUAL ON BEHALF OF VS. CEREBRAL MEDICAL GROUP, P.A., A PROFESSIONAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 22, 2025, line 2. COMPLIANCE HEARING. This matter is on calendar for a compliance hearing following entry of the Final Order Approving Class Settlement, signed October 5, 2023. Good cause appearing, the compliance hearing is CONTINUED TO June 18 at 9:00 am in this Department. The parties to submit a declaration from the Settlement Administrator and a declaration or memorandum regarding any outstanding issues no later than June 9, 2025, with courtesy copy of all filings promptly delivered to this Department. Plaintiff must lodge a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on May 22, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZAHIR NASERI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, And For Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 22, 2025, line 10. PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, And For Sanctions Against Defendant. Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses, filed March 28, 2025, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to the parties via email. Prior to the hearing, Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org an updated proposed order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: YUET PENG CHEONG VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 22, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANTS META PLATFORMS, INC., MARYAM GHOLAMI, MATT STEINER, SANDEEP PANDEY'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for gender/sex harassment is overruled. The alternative motion to strike is denied. As explained in the companion motion to compel arbitration and stay, plaintiff sufficiently pleads a claim for gender/sex harassment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: YUET PENG CHEONG VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 22, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANTS META PLATFORMS, INC., MARYAM GHOLAMI, MATT STEINER, SANDEEP PANDEY'S Motion For Protective Order. Defendant's motion for a protective order is denied. Plaintiff states a claim for sexual/gender harassment and there is no basis to compel arbitration of any of plaintiff's claims under the EFAA. Defendant's responses to the propounded discovery are due by June 23, 2025. Parties to meet and confer regarding scheduling the depositions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
